title: phaca intonsa
intro: Phaca intonsa, also known as the Indian clover, is a perennial legume native to tropical Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, cough, and digestive issues. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers are rich in flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which contribute to its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Phaca intonsa include infusions, decoctions, and powdered extracts, often used to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal problems.
